sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Levante UD is in poor form, having lost their last 3 matches and sitting at the bottom of the league table with 0 points. Real Betis, with a mid-table position (10th) and 5 points, shows mixed results with 1 win, 2 draws, and 1 loss in their last 4 league games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 5 head-to-head matchups, Real Betis has dominated with 3 wins compared to Levante's 2. The most recent encounters saw high-scoring games, with Betis winning 4-2 and 3-1, indicating a trend of goals on both sides.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Levante UD struggles offensively and defensively, ranking low in goals scored (15th) and conceded (17th), with poor possession stats (20th). Real Betis, while not exceptional, shows more balance in attack and defense, with a slightly better goal-scoring record.

sections.subtitle: Key Trends
sections.text: Both teams often see goals in their matches, with 'both teams scoring' occurring in 5 of Betis’ last 6 games and 5 of the last 6 head-to-heads. Levante’s defense has been leaky, without a clean sheet in their last 3 matches.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half summary
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between Levante UD and Real Betis at the Ciudad de Valencia ended with a scoreline of 2-1 in favor of Levante. Levante took the lead early through Iván Romero and added a second via Etta Eyong, showing clinical finishing despite having less possession. Real Betis managed to pull one back late in the half with a goal from Cucho Hernández, keeping themselves in the contest. The game has been competitive, with both sides having moments of dominance, though Betis controlled the ball for the majority of the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and possession
halftimeSections.text: Real Betis dominated possession in the first half, holding the ball for a significant portion of the time and creating more entries into the penalty area compared to Levante. Their ability to maintain control in the midfield allowed them to dictate the tempo at times. However, Levante made the most of their limited possession, converting their chances with efficiency. Levante’s defensive structure held firm against Betis’ pressure, though they will need to remain vigilant as Betis showed intent to attack frequently.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The attacking momentum in the first half swung between both teams at different stages. Early on, Levante showed bursts of attacking intent, particularly in the opening minutes when they took the lead. As the half progressed, Real Betis began to assert themselves with consistent spells of pressure, especially in the middle and latter parts of the half. Levante had their moments of resurgence, especially towards the end of the half, but Betis maintained more sustained attacking presence overall. These shifts suggest a dynamic game where both teams are capable of creating dangerous moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Levante, Iván Romero and Etta Eyong were pivotal in the first half, each finding the back of the net and posing a constant threat upfront. Pablo Martínez also played a crucial role with an assist and a key pass, showing creativity in the midfield. On the Real Betis side, Cucho Hernández stood out with his late goal, proving to be a danger man in the final third. Players like Héctor Bellerín and Antony also contributed with key passes, helping Betis build attacks despite not converting all their chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: Levante will need to focus on gaining more control over possession in the second half to prevent Betis from overwhelming them with sustained pressure. Their defense has been solid, but they must avoid giving away cheap opportunities in the final third. Real Betis, on the other hand, should look to capitalize on their dominance in possession by being more clinical in front of goal. They’ve created several chances and hit the woodwork, so sharpening their finishing will be key to turning the game in their favor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be an intense battle as Real Betis are likely to push hard for an equalizer, given their attacking intent and possession advantage in the first half. Levante will aim to protect their lead, potentially relying on counter-attacks to exploit any gaps left by Betis’ forward push. If Betis can maintain their momentum and convert their chances, they stand a good chance of leveling the score. However, Levante’s efficiency in front of goal makes them dangerous on the break, and they could extend their lead if given space.
